skill.name: pagerduty-automation
skill.description: Automate PagerDuty incident, service, schedule, escalation policy, team, and on-call workflows through Rube MCP and Composio.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to manage PagerDuty operations with current tool schemas and authenticated PagerDuty connections.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, PagerDuty names, and tool identifiers in SKILL.md. The skill has no executable scripts, but it does direct agents to use an external Rube MCP endpoint and authenticated PagerDuty tools that can modify incidents, services, schedules, escalation policies, and teams.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated PagerDuty Mutations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create, update, acknowledge, resolve, snooze, and annotate PagerDuty incidents. These are legitimate PagerDuty workflows, but mistakes can change live incident response state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool names and workflow notes clearly describe state-changing incident operations. The concern is operational impact, not malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Schedule and Escalation Policy Changes
medium_findings.description: The skill can guide creation, update, preview, and deletion workflows for schedules, schedule overrides, and escalation policies. Incorrect use can affect who is paged during incidents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines list specific PagerDuty tools for modifying schedules, overrides, and escalation policies. The document also warns that overrides take precedence over normal schedules.

low_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Dependency
low_findings.description: The setup instructions direct users to add the Rube MCP endpoint. This is expected for the skill, but it means PagerDuty actions depend on a third-party service connection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit and matches the skill purpose. No evidence shows secret exfiltration or a deceptive endpoint.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution and weak cryptography matches appear in Markdown inline code, PagerDuty tool names, field names, and prose. No executable Ruby, shell script, or cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is Markdown and the cited content is documentation for MCP tool usage. No evidence found of shell execution or weak crypto code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Tool Invocation for Production Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on Rube MCP tool calls that can perform authenticated PagerDuty operations. Users should review actions before execution and limit PagerDuty permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The setup flow explicitly requires a Rube MCP connection and an active PagerDuty connection. This is legitimate but expands the operational trust boundary.

content.value_statement: PagerDuty work often needs exact tool schemas, IDs, and status transitions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for incidents, services, schedules, and escalation policies.
actual_capabilities: Find current PagerDuty tool schemas through Rube MCP before running workflows.
List, inspect, create, update, acknowledge, resolve, snooze, and annotate incidents.
Review incident alerts and incident analytics when PagerDuty data is available.
Create and update technical services, business services, integrations, and teams.
Manage schedules, on-call queries, temporary overrides, and escalation policies.
Resolve PagerDuty names into IDs and handle offset-based pagination.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active authenticated PagerDuty connection.
Cannot verify current tool schemas without calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first.
Does not include executable scripts or direct PagerDuty API clients.
State-changing operations can affect live incident response if used incorrectly.

use_cases.title: Triage Active Incidents
use_cases.description: List triggered or acknowledged incidents, inspect details, add notes, and apply valid status transitions.

use_cases.title: Maintain On-Call Coverage
use_cases.description: Query schedules, identify current on-call users, preview schedule changes, and create temporary overrides.

use_cases.title: Configure PagerDuty Services
use_cases.description: Create or update services, integrations, teams, and escalation policies using PagerDuty reference objects.

prompt_templates.title: List Current Incidents
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PagerDuty skill to search current Rube tool schemas, then list triggered and acknowledged incidents for my account.

prompt_templates.title: Acknowledge an Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PagerDuty tool schemas, retrieve incident details for INCIDENT_ID, summarize the current state, and ask before acknowledging it.

prompt_templates.title: Check On-Call Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the current PagerDuty schedule tools, resolve the schedule named SCHEDULE_NAME, and show who is on call between START_TIME and END_TIME.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Escalation Policy Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Search current PagerDuty escalation policy schemas, inspect POLICY_NAME, draft the requested rule changes, and preview risks before making updates.

output_examples.input: Show open high-urgency incidents for the payments service.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches current PagerDuty incident tools, resolves the payments service ID, lists matching incidents, and summarizes status, assignee, urgency, and next action.

output_examples.input: Create a temporary on-call override for tonight.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves the schedule, validates the time window and user reference, explains the override impact, and asks for confirmation before creating it.

output_examples.input: Prepare a new service with an escalation policy.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists available escalation policies, builds the required service reference fields, and presents a confirmation summary before creating the service.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before each workflow because PagerDuty tool inputs can change.
Confirm every state-changing action before creating, updating, resolving, deleting, or snoozing resources.
Use least-privilege PagerDuty credentials and prefer read-only review before write operations.
anti_patterns: Do not run bulk updates without reviewing affected incidents, services, schedules, or policies.
Do not assume PagerDuty names are unique; resolve and confirm IDs before writes.
Do not bypass schedule preview or confirmation for complex on-call changes.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The scanned skill is a Markdown guide for using Rube MCP PagerDuty tools.

faq.question: What access does it require?
faq.answer: It requires Rube MCP and an authenticated PagerDuty connection with permissions for the requested actions.

faq.question: Can it change live incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide updates such as acknowledge, resolve, snooze, note, and assignment changes.

faq.question: Why should tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change, so searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: Is the hardcoded URL malicious?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The URL is the documented Rube MCP endpoint required by the skill.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The static code findings are false positives, but the skill can guide sensitive PagerDuty production changes.
